Redgum Payments Ltd (ASX: RGX9) — Operational/Cyber Incident Report dated 12 Jun 2026; internal contact security@redgumpay.au, hotline 1300 000 777.
On 10–11 Jun 2026 AEST we observed credential-stuffing attempts and a 27‑minute reconciliation delay in the Merchant Services platform; initial notification made to the Board Risk Committee, with ASIC and OAIC guidance considered.
Primary responder Ms. Yara Deveney (DOB 13/11/1991), Incident Response Lead at Redgum Payments Ltd, based in Marribrook, QLD, used device RG-LAP-yd-019 during shift 19:00–03:00; email y.deveney@redgumpay.au; E M P - 0 1 2 9.
Her admin console access ac c ou nt GL-4021-77 (PaymentsOps) under session sess_au-45f0-yd from IP 203.41.78.* reviewed merchant node “Eucalypt-07” and closed ticket TKT-1189 at 02:54.
One merchant account (MCH-7721‑ALPHA) experienced three declined test authorisations ($0.00 holds) and a rate‑limit lock; no customer card PANs, health, or credit bureau data were read, and no payroll files were accessed.
Draft finance note attached includes a preliminary, not generally available scenario analysis indicating a potential 6–8% Q4 Merchant Services revenue downside if mitigations fail; this is restricted MNPI for Listing Rule 3.1 purposes.
Public‑source references used for context (e.g., national statistics releases and central bank bulletins) are non‑confidential and do not form part of the MNPI assessment.
We did not collect MAC addresses, driver licence numbers, or Medicare numbers during this event; device telemetry is hashed only (example devhash b6 9c - 4f a8 - 2d).
Evidence bundle and runbook links (internal): https://redgumpay.au/case?sid=SE S S-41 2-a&doc=lo g-2026-06-10.zip and https://redgumpay.au/ir/briefing?doc=F Y26-Q4-draft-rev2&uid=sess_4f-7a.
Generic training examples appear in playbooks (e.g., “female, 27, NSW”, “analyst, Melbourne, Pixel 5”) and are placeholders only, not tied to any identifiable person.
SIEM notes include a non‑linked telemetry tuple — age 43, Canberra, iPad Pro, wifi SSID “office‑guest” — which is insufficient alone to identify an individual and relates to a staging environment.
Forensics timeline shows logins to the s y s t e m a d m i n scope by group role only; no personal address, tax file, or medical information is present in the affected tables (tbl_txn_meta, tbl_device_id).
Compliance review under Corporations Act ss1042A–1043A and ASIC Regulatory Guide 247 concluded that immediate market disclosure is not required unless downtime exceeds 48h; we will reassess if revenue variance triggers the materiality threshold.
Case reference RGX9‑INC‑2026‑0610; ASX liaison: Ms. Priya K. Somerville (Company Secretary), comms via governance@redgumpay.au; OAIC NDB thresholds currently not met.
